title: Duanwu Festival Celebrations Underway in China
description:
Saturday marks China's traditional Duanwu Festival, or the Dragon Boat Festival. People across China are celebrating the holiday with various folk activities. 

Dragon boat racing is an indispensable part of the festival. The boats are so named because the fore and stern are in the shape of a dragon. In the Peach Garden Lake scenic spot in east China's Anhui Province, dragon boat racing has developed into an exciting tournament that attracts tens of thousands of spectators every year. 

Fourteen teams took part in the competition this year. The rowers strive to paddle following the drumbeats in order to go faster. All participants were amateurs from local and nearby counties. Compared to the result of the competition, they enjoy more on the process of racing. 

"I think the atmosphere is great. Everyone is so happy. Whether we win or lose is not the point. To feel happy is the most important thing," rower Zhai Chen said. 

The intense competition lured many visitors. Gao Hongyu, a tourist from Anhui's Ma An Shan city, said she thought this tradition is a reflection of Chinese spirit.

"I came here with my 55 friends today to watch the competition. China is a "land of dragon" and we always say Chinese people are descendants of dragon. This dragon boat racing before the festival has strong ethnic characteristics," she said. 

In east China's Jiangxi Province, people are celebrating the holiday with another form of activity: Caijie (street walk). More than 1,000 people paraded in over 30 formations, holding various folk custom goods. 

The Duanwu Festival or Dragon Boat Festival is one of the most important traditional holidays in China. It is celebrated every year on the fifth day of the fifth month of the Chinese lunar calendar.

title: Living Heritage: Duanwu
description:
Although tales of its origin differ, the two-millennia-old festival is widely celebrated in memory of Qu Yuan, a patriotic poet during the Warring States Period (475-221 BC). When Qu realized his country's decline was beyond recovery, his remorse grew so strong that he committed suicide, on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month. Legend has it that locals rowed boats to save the poet and threw zongzi, or sticky rice dumplings, into the river to keep fish from devouring his body. This tradition was passed down in China, even travelling across borders, carrying China’s respect for a patriot to all parts of the world.

A host of other practices are also associated with Duanwu. The fifth day of the fifth month is considered unlucky and poisonous. To rid themselves of the misfortune, many people, especially those in South China, take herbal baths, hang moxa and calamus plants over their doors and wear sachets stuffed with Chinese herbs, all with strong scents, to do away with venomous insects. Realgar wine, a common antidote against venom, is often enjoyed along with meals. Five-colored thread bracelets are also worn to obviate evil spirits. 

Duanwu began as a day to remember a poet steeped with love for his country. His patriotism is still celebrated today, with rich and fun customs that unite hearts across the globe, in methods that hail to the harmony between nature and man.

title: The Dragon Boat Festival (Duanwu Festival) is a traditional Chinese festival  #dragonboatfestival
description:
The Dragon Boat Festival (Duanwu Festival), is a traditional Chinese holiday that takes place on the 5th day of the 5th month of the lunar calendar. In 2024, this would fall on June 10th. 

The origins of the Dragon Boat Festival date back over 2,000 years and it remains an important cultural celebration across Chinese communities worldwide. Many cities and regions hold large-scale dragon boat races and other festivities to mark the occasion.

The main traditions of the Dragon Boat Festival include:
1) Dragon boat racing - Teams race narrow boats decorated with dragon heads and tails. This commemorates the legendary suicide of the ancient Chinese poet Qu Yuan.
2) Eating zongzi - These are glutinous rice dumplings wrapped in bamboo or reed leaves. The shape and wrapping is symbolic.
3) Hanging wormwood and calamus - These herbs are believed to have protective powers and ward off evil spirits during this time.
4) Drinking realgar wine - Realgar is a yellow sulfide mineral that was traditionally used to make a wine consumed during the festival.

title: Happy Duanwu Festival
description:
The Dragon Boat Festival is a traditional cultural festival popular in China and other countries in the cultural circle of Chinese characters. It is said that Qu Yuan, a poet of Chu during the Warring States Period, committed suicide by jumping on the Miluo River on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month. Later generations also regarded the Dragon Boat Festival as a festival to commemorate Qu Yuan; Cao E and Jie Zitui, etc. The origin of the Dragon Boat Festival covers the ancient astrological culture, humanistic philosophy and other aspects, and contains profound and rich cultural connotations. In the inheritance and development, it is mixed with a variety of folk customs. Due to different regional cultures, there are customs and details in different places. difference.

title: DIY Dragon Boat Festival (Duanwu Jie)  Good Luck Bracelet，Five Color String Wish Bracelet
description:
Dragon Boat Festival is one of the oldest holidays in China, said to date back nearly 2,500 years to the Warring States Period. It is held the fifth day of the fifth month each lunar year, and has several possible origins. By far the most popular tale is that of Qu Yuan.

A common activity during the Dragon Boat Festival is to tie on a bracelet/anklet made of five different coloured strings. The bracelet is worn until the first rain following the event, at which time it is thrown into a river — presumably to resemble a dragon on the river. So today let's learn how to tie such a Dragon Boat Festival bracelet！

這是去年接用剩的午時水，端午節磁場陽氣特別旺，不管水放多久，水依然乾淨清澈。
善意提醒，水久放只適合外用，不適合飲用，若要飲用，端午當天，午時接水，直接煮壺午時水，喝下肚，旺讓自己成為昭財體。
有人問午時水怎麼用？端午節的中午11點到下午1點之間，打開家裡水龍頭拿罐子接起來就對了。
有老師教用午時水煮財水，放財位旺財，以前我也會特地，拿午時水去煮財水，現在生活太忙，加上家裡電磁爐不好煮開，索性就不煮了！
其實，不煮財水也完全沒關係！拿來『外用』更方便。最棒的方法就是加熱水做成陰陽水，加艾草、抹草、芙蓉洗澡。如果覺得運勢卡卡、能量低迷的人，端午中午一定要接，洗洗澡幫自己除穢、洗掉負能量，氣場直接煥然一新！
如果想加強，午時水的能量，可以將午時水，放置在室外，曬三天的日夜，或七天的日夜，接受白天陽光，和晚上月光的曝曬。
看完這支影片，現在立刻去廚房找一個空瓶子，鬧鐘定好，11點一到馬上開水龍頭！不用煮、不麻煩，錯過就要再等一年喔！快分享給你最重要的家人朋友，提醒他們，一起接午時水！

title: Happy Dragon Boat Festival 2013 - Duanwu Festival
description:
On June 12, Google celebrates the Dragon Boat Festival with a nice Doodle.

Duanwu Festival, also known as Dragon Boat Festival and the Double Fifth, is a traditional and statutory holiday originating in China and associated with a number of East Asian and Southeast Asian societies. 
The festival occurs on the 5th day of the 5th month of the lunisolar Chinese calendar. This is the source of the alternative name of Double Fifth. The date varies from year to year on the Gregorian calendar. In 2011, this fell on June 6 and in 2012 on June 23. The focus of the celebrations includes eating rice dumplings zongzi, drinking realgar wine xionghuangjiu, and racing dragon boats.
The sun, like the Chinese dragon, traditionally represents masculine energy, whereas the moon, like the phoenix, traditionally represents feminine energy. The masculine image of the dragon is thus naturally associated.

title: 【端午節特別企劃】開箱宜蘭限定超市！喜互惠獨有的櫻桃鴨粽是什麼味道？feat林國漳、韓瑩
description:
宜蘭限定的好味道，真的「勁好呷」！

端午節前夕，我來到宜蘭，和林國漳律師、韓瑩一起逛一間宜蘭人最熟悉，但很多外地朋友可能沒聽過的在地超市：喜互惠。

對宜蘭人來說，喜互惠就像生活裡的「好厝邊」。平常買生鮮蔬果、生活用品，到了端午節前，粽子、伴手禮、農漁特產、特色熟食，也都能在這裡一次準備齊全。

這次跟著國漳和韓瑩一起採買，才知道喜互惠裡藏著這麼多宜蘭好味道。從端午節必吃的櫻桃鴨粽、農會豆奶、牛舌餅，到三星蔥餅和各式農漁特產，每一樣都充滿宜蘭特色，也讓人感受到在地產業的用心。

採買結束後，我們也來到林國漳律師的事務所，一起享用今天買到的宜蘭美食。端午連假快到了，最適合和朋友相聚、分享粽子與家鄉味。

祝福大家端午節安康，平安順心！

title: Friendship Through Sport — AFL Duanwu Festival 2025
description:
Friendship Through Sport — AFL Duanwu Festival 2025
Video Report by Bing Lin | 31 May 2025

Presented by MA Financial Group, the third annual AFL Duanwu Festival brought together nearly 400 political and business leaders in Sydney to celebrate tradition and strengthen Australia–China friendship.

With its theme “Friendship Through Sport, Celebration Through Duanwu”, the event fused the energy of Aussie Rules with the spirit of the Dragon Boat Festival — creating a powerful platform for cultural exchange.

Keynote speakers included Governor-General Sam Mostyn, Chinese Ambassador Xiao Qian, and Minister Don Farrell, MP Jason Yat-Sen Li, and National President of the Australia China Business Council David Olsson, all of whom underscored the festival’s vital role in fostering grassroots connections between the two nations.

This special report also features an exclusive interview with festival founder Richard Colless, sports legend and former Sydney Swans Chairman, as he reflects on the role of sport in connecting cultures.

以球会友，共贺端午——2025澳式橄榄球端午庆典特别报道
视频报道：林冰  
2025.05.31
第三届“澳式橄榄球端午庆典”在悉尼圆满举办，由美驰集团（MA Financial Group）倾力呈现，近400位政商界嘉宾共襄盛举，庆佳节、促友谊。活动以“以球会友·共贺端午”为主题，融合澳式橄榄球与中华文化，成为中澳交流的重要桥梁。

澳大利亚总督萨姆·莫斯廷、中国驻澳大使肖千、联邦贸易与旅游部长唐·法雷尔、国会议员李逸仙以及澳中商会全国主席大卫·奥尔森等发表致辞，凸显活动在推动民间交流中的深远意义。

本期特别专访本次活动创始人、澳大利亚体育界传奇人物——原悉尼天鹅队主席（任期20年）、MA Financial资产管理主席Richard Colless ，请他分享天鹅队历程、他对中澳体育文化交流的深厚情感与推动愿景。

title: Beijing acoge evento de intercambio cultural con motivo de la Fiesta del Bote del Dragón
description:
Con motivo de la tradicional Fiesta del Bote del Dragón, también conocida como Duanwu, se celebró el evento de intercambio cultural "Botes de dragón cargados de afecto: celebrando juntos el Duanwu" a orillas del río Tonghui, en el poblado de Gaobeidian, en Beijing.

Los asistentes compartieron la tradición de elaborar los zongzi, tamales chinos de arroz glutinoso envueltos en hojas de bambú, y disfrutaron de espectáculos folclóricos, como el diábolo y las exhibiciones sobre zancos. Asimismo, los visitantes extranjeros tuvieron la oportunidad de participar en una regata de botes de dragón, guiados por entrenadores profesionales, lo que aportó dinamismo y un ambiente festivo a esta jornada. #China #CulturaChina #HC #FiestaDelBoteDelDragón

title: Panda Yin Ke (银柯) celebrates Duanwu (The Dragon Boat Festival)
description: Yin Ke, the panda living now in Lin Yi (临沂), recently spent Duanwu with tourists. Duanwu is a traditional festival of China, and it is said that people celebrate it in memory of Qu Yuan (屈原), a patriotic poet who jumped into the river for the reason that he didn't want to witness the demise of his motherland. In this video, we can clearly see a dragon boat, and there is some of Yinke's favourite food in the boat, like bamboo leaves and carrots etc., they are wrapped like the traditional zongzi of this festival. [The Story of Pearl Girl 珠帘玉幕] After enduring countless hardships, Hepu's pearl diver Duanwu escapes the brutal pearl farm and crosses paths with Yan Zijing, a merchant from the Western Regions, and Zhang Jinran, a noble gentleman. Joining Yan's trade caravan, she fights her way up, lured by the glittering world of jewels within reach. But on the journey northwest, she unearths secrets of her own past and is drawn into Yan Zijing's complex web of revenge, losing loved ones and facing betrayal. Reborn as Su Muzhe, she ventures into Yangzhou, determined to seek the truth no matter the cost. With a clear mind free from hatred, she founds her own business, "Clear Mind," and joins forces with Zhang Jinran to clear an old injustice. Yan Zijing, once intent on shouldering his shame and revenge alone, eventually finds peace and braves life's storms with her. As the dust settles, Su Muzhe reaches new heights at the Jewel Exhibition, inspiring countless women who once yielded to fate to reclaim their own paths.

Starring: Zhao Lusi / Liu Yuning / Tang Xiaotian / Shang Xinyue / Xie Keyin / Tang Zhenchao
主演：赵露思 / 刘宇宁 / 唐晓天 / 尚新月 / 谢可寅 / 唐振超

title: Τι είναι το Duanwu ?  端午节词汇 Words about Dragon Boat Festival-Το Κινεζικα Φεστιβάλ Σκαφών Δράκων
description:
端午节，也叫端阳节、龙舟节，在中国农历每年的五月初五，是中国的四大传统节日之一
（还有春节、清明节和中秋节）。

端午节是为了纪念古代著名爱国诗人屈原而来的。很久很久以前，爱国诗人屈原投河自尽 ，人们为了纪念他，用粽叶或竹叶包裹糯米投入江中给屈原食用。同时，为了怕水中的鱼
儿吃掉粽子， 人们通过赛龙舟的方式赶走鱼儿。

Το Φεστιβάλ Σκαφών Δράκων (Dragon Boat Festival) γίνεται κάθε χρόνο, σύμφωνα με το σεληνιακό ημερολόγιο,κάθε 5 Μαιου.
Είναι ένα από τα τέσσερα παραδοσιακά φεστιβάλ της Κίνας.

(Γιορτή της Άνοιξης, ChingMing "γιορτή στην μνήμη των προγόνων" φεστιβαλ, Μεσο-φθινοπωρινό φεστιβάλ).

Το φεστιβάλ Σκαφών Δράκων (Dragon Boat Festival) είναι γι ανα τιμήσουν τον αρχαίο διάσημο πατριώτη ποιητή Qu Yuan.Τα αρχαία εκείνα χρόνια ο ποιητής Qu Yuan αυτοκτόνησε πέφτωντας στον ποταμό.
Για να τιμήσουν οι άνθρωποι τον ποιητή Qu Yuan έφτιαχναν νταμπλινγκς με γλασέ,κολλώδες ρύζι,τυλιγμένο σε φύλλα από μπαμπου και το έριχναν στο ποτάμι για τον Qu Yuan.Επίσης για να φοβήσουν τα ψάρια που θα πήγαιναν στον Qu Yuan και να φύγουν.

Τα παιδιά τρώνε νταμπλινγκ προς τιμή του ποιητή και για να διώξουν τα ψάρια για τον αγώνα με τα σκάφη.

    Zongzi (粽子) is a traditional Chinese rice dish made of glutinous ricestuffed with different fillings and wrapped in bamboo leaves, generally of the species Indocalamus tessellatus, sometimes, with reed leaves, or other large flat leaves. They are cooked by steaming or boiling. In the Western world, they are also known as rice dumplings or sticky rice dumplings.

title: A Cross-Cultural Duanwu Celebration in Shanghai!
description:
Celebrate Dragon Boat Festival (Duanwu Festival) with a twist! 🌿 This year, “Shanghai son-in-law” Alexander joins floral artist Huang Yujie to learn the ancient tradition of hanging mugwort—a practice dating back to the Shang and Zhou dynasties, believed to ward off evil and protect health.

Discover how traditional Chinese herbs like mugwort, calamus, and eupatorium are transformed into beautiful, fragrant bouquets that blend classical ritual with modern floral design. As Alexander crafts his own Duanwu bouquet, he explores the deeper meanings behind this festival and how age-old customs connect with everyday life today.

Come experience the charm of Duanwu Festival through the eyes of a foreign friend—where tradition meets creativity in the heart of Shanghai!

title: Dragon Boat Racing at the Duanwu Festival
description: Dragon boat racing is one of the customs associated with China's Duanwu Festival, as well as one of its most significant folk traditions. While it is widely practiced throughout southern China, the custom also exists in northern cities situated near rivers and lakes—though in these regions, it predominantly takes the form of "dry dragon boat" performances, a type of dragon boat dance.
caption: false

title: Duck Catching Contest Held in Southwest China for Duanwu Festival
description:
Dragon boat racing is not the only activity to be held during the three-day Duanwu Festival (Dragon Boat Festival) as locals in Zhenyuan County of southwest China's Guizhou Province partake in a duck catching contest to celebrate the traditional Chinese festival. 

In the ancient town, ducks are sacred in they eye's of locals. Each Dragon Boat Festival, it is a tradition that the dragon boat racers engage in the lively duck catching contest after they have the dragon boat race.

During the duck catching contest, handlers will scatter the ducks across the river. The racers then jump into the water to catch the ducks. However, the ducks prove to be elusive creatures as they either fly or swim away quickly to avoid being caught.

According to local customs, whoever catches a duck will have good luck. More on: http://www.cctvplus.com/news/20160610/8023710.shtml#!language=1

歌曲《定军山》Mount Dingjun
As an original song, it was sung by the famous singer Tu Honggang, written by the widely-known lyricist Chen Tao, whose masterpieces includeFarewell to My ConcubineandRepay the Country with Supreme Loyalty, and Qian Lei, a composer who has produced a torrent of music works such asBig FishandLonely Warrior. The song also draws inspiration from the classic singing tune of the Peking OperaMount Dingjunof the Tan School. The program paints a vivid picture of the Three Kingdoms period and applauds loyalty and righteousness, bringing the embodiment of patriotism to the next level.

title: Celebrate the 2015 Duanwu Festival in Central Florida with CAACF
description:
very summer, Chinese people celebrate the Duanwu Festival 端午節 (Duānwǔ Jié), honoring traditions and legends that are now over 2000 years old. It’s an official public holiday scheduled on the fifth day of the fifth month in the lunar calendar, and you might hear it called the “Double Fifth Festival.” As its timing is based on the lunar calendar, the date for the festival on the Gregorian calendar that Americans use isn’t always the same. This year, the double fifth fell on June 20. Here in Orlando, the dragon boat team “C.H.A.R.G.E.” hosted a workshop in the spirit of the Duanwu Festival at the Orlando Rowing Club (ORC) on the sunny and steaming Saturday, June 27.

title: La fête de Duanwu
description:
La fête de #Duanwu, qui a lieu le cinquième jour du cinquième mois lunaire, tombe cette année le 14 juin. Appelée également la fête des bateaux-dragons, elle a été inscrite en 2009 par l'UNESCO sur la liste représentative du patrimoine culturel immatériel de l’humanité. Cette fête est également associée à la mémoire du poète Qu Yuan. Parmi les #coutumes et #activités marquantes qui ont lieu durant cette fête, on peut citer la course de bateaux-dragons, la dégustation de #Zongzi, un mets à base de riz gluant farci enveloppé dans une feuille de bambou, la consommation de vin soufré ainsi que la confection de petits sachets de tissu remplis d’une poudre censée protéger contre les maladies et chasser les mauvais esprits. En effet, les températures sont élevées durant cette période de l’année et les insectes susceptibles de transmettre virus et parasites sont très actifs. 
#龙舟 lóng zhōu : les bateaux-dragons
#粽子 zòng zi : les gâteaux de riz glutineux enveloppés dans des feuilles de bambou 
#香囊 xiāng náng : de petites sacoches remplies de poudre protectrice
#雄黄酒 xióng huáng jiǔ : le vin soufré

title: Duanwu — The Festival That Remembers Qu Yuan, the Meaning of Zongzi and Dragon Boats | Qu Yuan EP65
description:
Every year on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month.

China, Korea, Japan, and Vietnam observe Duanwu.

All of this began with the death of Qu Yuan.

In 278 BC, when Qu Yuan cast himself into the Miluo River, the villagers made rice cakes and threw them into the river. So that the fish would not devour his body.

These rice cakes are zongzi (粽子). Triangular pyramids wrapped in bamboo leaves.

Also, people boarded boats and rowed swiftly to search the river. To find his body. This was the origin of dragon boat (龍舟) racing.

Even now at Duanwu, we eat zongzi and watch dragon boat races.

For fifteen hundred years, every year, we remember the death of a single loyal minister.

One word Qu Yuan left for us.

"Sooner would I cast myself into the river than soil myself in the mud of the worldly."

It is extreme. We cannot follow it. But its spirit we can hold in our hearts.

Whatever way the world turns, let us guard one truth within ourselves.

Next Duanwu, when you take a bite of zongzi, think for a moment of Qu Yuan.

title: Fête de Duanwu
description:
Aujourd’hui, selon le calendrier lunaire chinois, nous célébrons le Festival des Bateaux-Dragons (端午节 – Duānwǔ Jié), qui tombe chaque année le 5e jour du 5e mois.
Dans cette vidéo, je vous fais découvrir deux traditions fascinantes liées à cette fête populaire en Chine :
🐉 Les courses de bateaux-dragons : très spectaculaires, elles sont organisées dans de nombreuses régions. Ici, on voit les préparatifs des équipes dans les étroites rivières de Foshan, près de Canton – une ambiance pleine d’énergie et de passion !
🍚Les zòngzi, ces délicieux petits paquets de riz gluant enveloppés dans des feuilles de bambou. Il en existe de nombreuses variantes selon les régions : sucrés ou salés. Personnellement, j’adore ceux garnis de pâte de haricots rouges et de jujube (une datte chinoise) 😋
Enfin, la vidéo met aussi en valeur la beauté des caractères chinois anciens (en écriture oraculaire, dite « jiǎgǔwén »), en illustrant les mots dragon, bateau, Duanwu, etc. Une belle manière de montrer la richesse visuelle de la langue chinoise.
Cette fête est aussi l’occasion de souhaiter à ses proches santé et sérénité :
🌿 端午安康 (Duānwǔ ānkāng) 
– "Que cette fête vous apporte paix et santé !"
Connaissiez-vous cette fête traditionnelle chinoise ? 😊
#FêteDesBateauxDragons #CultureChinoise #Zongzi #Duānwǔ #DécouverteCulturelle#caractereschinois#汉字之美#kreegachinois#apprendissageduchinois

title: 2022端午奇妙游  LA FÊTE DES BATEAUX-DRAGON #DUANWU #端午节, UNE DES FÊTES TRADITIONNELLES CHINOISES
description:
女子群舞。舞蹈取材《诗经 郑风 野有蔓草》。节目刻画了青年男女在清晨的田间小路不期而遇产生爱情萌芽，以及随之而来的“一日不见，如三秋兮”的辗转反侧、思慕美人的甜蜜与苦涩交织的复杂情感体验，展现含蓄婉转的中国古典爱情美学。

歌声悠扬，舞姿妙曼。充分展现中国女子古典舞之美。

It’s a women’s group dance inspired byThe Creeping Grass, Songs Collected in Zheng, The Book of Songs. It describes a young boy coming across a young girl on a country road at dawn. They fall in love with each other as the seed of romance inevitably takes root in their joyful hearts.

Featuring an enjoyable melody and graceful movements and postures, the show fully embodies the beauty of Chinese women in a classical dance.

description:
¿Qué tienen en común las carreras de botes dragón, los zongzi y un poeta que vivió hace más de 2.000 años?

En este episodio exploramos el Festival Duanwu (端午节), una de las celebraciones tradicionales más importantes de China. Conoceremos la historia de Qu Yuan, el origen de las carreras de botes dragón, las tradiciones gastronómicas del festival y los valores culturales que han mantenido viva esta celebración hasta nuestros días.

Acompáñenos a descubrir una tradición que combina historia, memoria, comunidad y patrimonio cultural.

"The faster the drums beat, the farther the spirits flee." The world's first AIGC-powered animated series on Chinese civilization, CHINESE STORIES, premieres its latest episode today: “Dragon Boat Racing at Duanwu.”

Guided by BaiZe, the legendary beast from the Classic of Mountains and Seas, the episode delves deep into the soul-stirring legend of Qu Yuan, the Warring States poet who cast himself into the Miluo River in a final act of loyalty and despair.

Through poetic visuals and digital myth-making, the story resurrects an ancient belief: hat speed could ward off evil, and that the pounding of drums and the surge of boats were more than rituals — they were an echo of grief, a race against darkness, and a timeless tribute to the spirit of sacrifice.

title: 端午节 Duan Wu Jie
description:
Hoy celebramos el Duan Wu Jie, también conocido como el Dragon Boat Festival. Este día, lleno de historia y tradición, nos recuerda la leyenda del poeta Qu Yuan y su sacrificio por amor a su país. 🌟📜

Según la historia, Qu Yuan, un respetado poeta y ministro, se lanzó al río Miluo en protesta contra la corrupción en su país. Los aldeanos, en un esfuerzo por salvarlo, remaron en sus botes al ritmo de los tambores, lo que dio origen a las emocionantes carreras de barcos de dragón que celebramos hoy. 🚣‍♂️🐉

Además de las vibrantes carreras, el Duan Wu Jie se celebra con la preparación y disfrute de los deliciosos 粽子 (zongzi). Estos paquetes de arroz glutinoso envueltos en hojas de bambú pueden llevar rellenos dulces o salados y se cocinan en agua caliente o al vapor. Se disfrutan tanto como comida principal como merienda. 🍚🍃

Los zongzi no solo son sabrosos, sino que también representan el esfuerzo de los aldeanos por mantener a los peces y espíritus malignos alejados del río donde Qu Yuan se lanzó, arrojando estos paquetes al agua. 🌊✨

Hoy, os invitamos a uniros a nuestra celebración y a descubrir cómo se preparan estos tradicionales zongzi en nuestro video. 🎉

端午節，也是一年之中陽氣最旺、能量最強的時刻。

當太陽走到正中央，也就是「日正財神到」的黃金轉運點。

掛艾草，是擋災；佩香囊，是辟穢；門灑鹽，是除穢迎新，而老一輩說，這天正財神也會到，給認真生活的人送財送福。

所以今天，送你最實在的兩樣：財和平安。

願你荷包萬貫：不是一夜暴富，是踏踏實實，日日都有進帳，月月都有結餘。

願你身帶平安福：這份福氣，跟著你出門，跟著你回家，跟著你過完一整個夏天。

財來了，平安在了，日子自然就美好了。

願你日日有進帳，萬事皆美好，端午安康。

title: 龍舟競技端午節 (中文版) Dragon Boat Racing at Duanwu (Chinese version)
description:
「鼓聲越急，邪祟愈遠！」《CHINESE STORIES》「龍舟競渡端午節」以《山海經》神獸白澤為引，深度還原戰國詩人屈原投江殉國的悲壯史詩，重現古人「以速度驅邪」的原始信仰。

"The faster the drums beat, the farther the spirits flee." 

Guided by BaiZe, the legendary beast from the Classic of Mountains and Seas, "Dragon Boat Racing at Duanwu" from 《CHINESE STORIES》delves deep into the soul-stirring legend of Qu Yuan, the Warring States poet who drown himself into the Miluo River in a final act of loyalty and despair. 

Through poetic visuals and digital myth-making, the story resurrects an ancient belief: speed could ward off evil and that the pounding of drums and the surge of boats were more than rituals — they were an echo of grief, a race against darkness, and a timeless tribute to the spirit of sacrifice.

title: Duanwu afternoon tea - NZ Han Culture Association
description:
On the fifth day of the fifth month in the lunar calendar, during the Dragon Boat Festival, the fragrance of rice wafts through the air, and the aroma of bamboo leaves filled with glutinous rice drifts around. It's said that as Duanwu arrives, the weather gets clearer and the days grow longer in the middle of summer. However, for us in Auckland, it's quite the opposite. This year, the winter days in the southern hemisphere are unusually cold, but that hasn't dampened the enthusiasm of fellow countrymen coming to join the tea gathering.

In the traditional customs of the Dragon Boat Festival since ancient times, in addition to the deliciousness of zongzi and the fragrance of mugwort, there's also the subtle and elegant scent of sachets. Legend has it that the fifth day of the fifth lunar month is a poisonous day, and wearing sachets on this day can safeguard one's health and ward off bad luck. Creating sachets is also a significant part of this tea gathering. These small sachets carry thousands of years of traditional culture, traversing centuries of history. Through these sachets, one can feel the graceful and composed way of life of ancient people and their wishes for a smooth and healthy life.

❤️ Many thanks to @玉川°有茶 (Yuchuan Tea) for their hard work in providing us with such exquisite tea and treats, as well as an insightful explanation of tea culture. Also, thanks to the collaborating photography studio @馨心摄影工作室 (Xinxin Photography Studio) for the photography! See you at the next event!

农历五月初五，端午时节，稻香满溢，粽叶飘香。都说端阳临中夏，时清日复长。然而身处奥克兰的我们，却恰好相反。今年南半球的冬日异常寒冷，但却也抵挡不住同袍们来参加茶会的热情。

在自古以来的端午习俗中，除了有粽子的鲜美、艾草的清香，还有来自香囊的淡雅芬芳。传说农历五月初五为毒日，在这一天佩戴香囊可以守护健康，趋吉避凶。制作香囊也是本场茶会的重场戏。小小香囊承载着几千年的传统文化，穿越千年历史的香囊，感受着古人慢生活的优雅从容，对生活顺遂安康的祈盼。

❤️感谢@玉川°有茶 的小腐的辛苦付出为我们提供如此精美的茶果子和精彩的茶文化讲解，也感谢一直以来的合作摄影工作室@馨心摄影工作室 的拍摄！下次活动见啦！

title: Fête de Duanwu en Chine : quelque 690 millions de voyages de passagers attendus du 31 mai au 2 juin
description:
En Chine, on se prépare à un pic de voyages pendant les trois jours de vacances de la Fête de Duanwu, ou le Festival des bateaux-dragons. Le ministère chinois des Transports prévoit 690 millions de voyages de passagers entre samedi et lundi, soit une augmentation de 7,7 points de pourcentage en glissement annuel. Les trajets les plus populaires sont les déplacements routiers de courte distance et les trajets familiaux, mais aussi les trajets ferroviaires de moins de deux heures. Ce week-end coïncide aussi avec la Journée des Enfants, célébrée dimanche. Plusieurs destinations ont intégré des activités adaptées aux familles. Les villes organisatrices de courses de bateaux-dragons s'attendent à une affluence considérable. 
(Photo : VCG)

title: Chinese Festival Conversation Ep.3 | Dragon Boat Festival | Learn Duanwu Festival Dialogue
description:
Learn how to talk about the Dragon Boat Festival (Duanwu Festival) in natural Chinese!
In this video, you’ll hear a short and practical Chinese conversation about how people celebrate — including watching dragon boat races, eating zongzi (sticky rice dumplings), and hanging mugwort. 🐉

🎧 Practice with three speeds:
Normal · Slow · Fast — perfect for beginners and intermediate learners.

📘 Improve your listening, speaking, and vocabulary skills while learning more about Chinese culture and traditions.
Repeat after the speakers and enjoy learning authentic Chinese dialogues!

👉 Like, subscribe, and explore the full Chinese Festival Conversation Series!

description:

📺【珠帘玉幕 The Story of Pearl Girl】合浦采珠奴端午历经万难逃出吃人的珠场，邂逅了西域商人燕子京和谦谦君子张晋然。她加入燕氏商队，一路摸爬滚打，璀璨的珠宝世界仿佛近在咫尺。然而西北之行，她却发现了自己的身世之密，更卷入了燕子京的复仇谜局，至亲皆死，至爱背离。端午改名苏幕遮勇闯扬州，宁可万劫不复也要追寻真相。然她终心如明镜，不被仇恨裹挟，一手缔造明镜台，联合张晋然昭雪旧案。原打算独自背负污名和仇恨的燕子京最终亦缓解心结，与她共对疾风骤雨。尘埃落定，苏幕遮在赛宝会上勇攀珠宝艺术高峰，众多原本屈从于命运的女子也因她而焕然新生

title: Festival DUANWU à Bastille Paris 2025 | Fête traditionnelle chinoise, Culture & Gastronomie 粽子文化节
description:
🇨🇳✨ Découvrez la magie de la fête DUANWU au cœur de Paris ! ✨🇨🇳
Le 31 mai 2025, l’Association Culturelle Médiatique Franco-Chinoise (ACMFC) a organisé avec succès le Festival DUANWU - Fêtes & Saveurs de Chine au Marché de la Bastille (Paris 11e).
Ce grand événement populaire a rassemblé gastronomie, artisanat, culture et performances artistiques, attirant des milliers de Parisiens et touristes.

🎋 Qu’est-ce que le DUANWU ?
Connu sous le nom de Fête des Bateaux-Dragons ou Fête du Printemps, le DUANWU est une fête traditionnelle chinoise en l’honneur du poète Qu Yuan et de l’héritage culturel millénaire.

🥟 Au programme :
✅ Dégustation de cuisine chinoise traditionnelle : raviolis, thé, desserts
✅ Ateliers de fabrication de zongzi 粽子工坊
✅ Calligraphie, noms chinois, essayage de costumes hanfu
✅ Activités pour enfants et familles
✅ Performances artistiques de jeunes franco-chinois
✅ Ambiance festive & interculturelle

🎯 Un pont culturel entre la France et la Chine, célébré dans la convivialité et le partage.

📌 Organisé par : ACMFC - Association Culturelle Médiatique Franco-Chinoise
📍 Lieu : Marché de la Bastille, Paris 11e
📅 Date : 31 mai 2025

💬 Partagez en commentaire votre moment préféré !
🔔 Abonnez-vous pour suivre nos prochains événements culturels en France & en Chine.

title: Animals enjoy Duanwu Festival in east, northwest China's wildlife parks
description:
Animals in wildlife parks spent their Duanwu Festival by enjoying delicious special zongzi and having fun with visitors in east China's Shanghai Municipality and northwest China's Shaanxi Province.

Zongzi is a traditional Chinese food made of glutinous rice wrapped in bamboo leaves. People often enjoy it on the Duanwu Festival, or the Dragon Boat Festival, which falls on the fifth day of the fifth month on the Chinese lunar calendar.

In the Shanghai Wild Animal Park, animals had their own special zongzi to mark the holiday. At the panda house, breeders laid zongzi in the center of fresh bamboo shoots, and then put various kinds of fruits on them. However, pandas showed no interest to zongzi, but went straightly to their favorite bamboo shoots.

title: First & Second-Time Beginner「Duanwu Zongzi 」两只小白第一第二次包上海肉粽
description:
Next Monday is Duanwu, a traditional Chinese holiday to memorize our ancestor Qu Yuan. On that day we usually eat Zongzi made of reed/bamboo leaves, sticky rice, and pork. This is what we are making today (P.S. This is my first time.) I am inviting my friends to teach me how to make it. We are making Shanghainese style with pork and salty egg yolk. Over the years, different regions have their unique ingredients to put into Zongzi. 

INGREDIENTS
3 lbs Pork Belly marinated with 1/3 cup Soy Sauce, 1/3 Dark Soy Sauce, 1/3 Cup Oyster Sauce, 1/3 cup Cooking Wine, 1 tbsp Sugar overnight 
4 lbs Sticky Rice marinated with 1 cup Soy Sauce, 1 cup Dark Soy Sauce
Salty Egg Yolk
Reed leaves

title: Festival Duanwu dan Budaya Perahu Naga | 端午节与龙舟文化
description:
Nama : Muhammad Syarhul Al-Farizy
NIM : 25020774159

Festival Duanwu (端午节) dan Budaya Perahu Naga (龙舟文化) merupakan salah satu warisan budaya tradisional Tiongkok yang masih dilestarikan hingga saat ini.

Festival ini berkaitan dengan kisah penyair patriotik Qu Yuan (屈原). Untuk mengenangnya, masyarakat mengadakan perlombaan perahu naga (龙舟比赛) dan menikmati zongzi (粽子), makanan tradisional yang menjadi ciri khas Festival Duanwu.

Melalui budaya perahu naga, kita dapat mempelajari nilai-nilai penting seperti persatuan, kerja sama, semangat pantang menyerah, serta penghargaan terhadap sejarah dan budaya tradisional.

Sinopsis:
Duanwu, una joven esclava de Hepu, escapa de la brutalidad de la mina de perlas y, en su huida, conoce al comerciante Yan Zijing y al noble Zhang Jinran. Se une a la caravana de la familia Yan, soñando con un futuro brillante, pero pronto descubre oscuros secretos sobre su origen y se ve envuelta en la venganza de Yan Zijing. Tras perder a sus seres queridos y ser traicionada por su amor, Duanwu, ahora como Su Muzhe, decide enfrentarse a su destino en Yangzhou para descubrir la verdad. Con el tiempo, y sin dejarse llevar por el odio, funda la tienda "Espejo Claro"; y, junto a Zhang Jinran, destapan un antiguo caso de injusticia.Al final, Yan Zijing encuentra paz y enfrenta su destino junto a ella. En la Gran Exposición de Joyas, Su Muzhe alcanza la cima en el arte de la orfebrería, inspirando a muchas mujeres a transformar sus vidas y romper las cadenas de la resignación.

title: The most grand summer festival in China - Dragon Boat(Duanwu)  Festival中国夏季最隆重的节日——端午节
description:
This is the most grand summer festival in China - Dragon Boat(Duanwu) Festival. In China, Dragon Boat Festival, Chinese New Year, Tomb-Sweeping Day, and Mid-Autumn Festival are collectively known as the four major traditional folk festivals. In September 2009, UNESCO officially reviewed and approved the Dragon Boat Festival as a world intangible cultural heritage. It became China's first festival to be inscribed as world intangible cultural heritage.
这是中国夏季最隆重的节日——端午节。在中国，端午节与春节、清明节、中秋节并称为中国四大传统民俗节日，是传统文化传承千年所形成的。端午节被列入UNESCO的世界非物质文化遗产名录。在2009年9月，联合国教科文组织正式审议，并批准了中国端午节列入世界非物质文化遗产名录。它成为中国首个入选世界非物质文化遗产的节日。

[The Story of Pearl Girl 珠帘玉幕] After enduring countless hardships, Hepu's pearl diver Duanwu escapes the brutal pearl farm and crosses paths with Yan Zijing, a merchant from the Western Regions, and Zhang Jinran, a noble gentleman. Joining Yan's trade caravan, she fights her way up, lured by the glittering world of jewels within reach. But on the journey northwest, she unearths secrets of her own past and is drawn into Yan Zijing's complex web of revenge, losing loved ones and facing betrayal. Reborn as Su Muzhe, she ventures into Yangzhou, determined to seek the truth no matter the cost. With a clear mind free from hatred, she founds her own business, "Clear Mind," and joins forces with Zhang Jinran to clear an old injustice. Yan Zijing, once intent on shouldering his shame and revenge alone, eventually finds peace and braves life's storms with her. As the dust settles, Su Muzhe reaches new heights at the Jewel Exhibition, inspiring countless women who once yielded to fate to reclaim their own paths.

Budak penyelam mutiara di Hepu, Duanwu, dengan susah payah berhasil keluar dari wilayah mutiara yang mematikan. Dia bergabung dengan rombongan pedagang Keluarga Yan dan melalui latihan yang keras selama perjalanan. Dunia permata yang berkilauan pun seolah-olah sudah dekat. Akan tetapi, saat perjalanan menuju barat laut, dia malah menemukan rahasia hidupnya dan terlibat dalam misteri pembalasan dendam Yan Zijing, semua kerabatnya pun meninggal dan dirinya dikhianati oleh orang yang dia cintai. title: Duanwu mit Grillfest 2025 端午包粽子烧烤
description:
Am 31. Mai 2025, nach dem Unterricht in der Chinesischen Sprachschule, feierten wir das Duanwu-Fest mit Grillen sowie gemeinsamer Reisknödeleinwicklung.
Beim Einwickeln von Reisknödeln mit Bambusblättern, beim Kochen der Reisknödel, beim Essen der Reisknödel, beim Grillen, nahmen wieder mehr als 100 Personen teil. 
Im Anschluss danach hörten wir den Vortrag - „Dilemmas and Chancen für die in Deutschland lebenden Chinesen“ von Frau Shaohua Dong, der alle dazu bringt, über das Leben nachzudenken, das der deutschen Denkweise näherkommt.
Das Drachenbootfest ist eines der vier traditionellen Feste der Han-Chinesen, zusammen mit dem Frühlingsfest, dem Qingming-Fest und dem Mittherbstfest. Seit der Antike wird das Drachenbootfest mit festlichen Aktivitäten wie Drachenbootrennen und dem Essen von Zongzi (Reisknödeln mit Bambusblätern) gefeiert. 
Wir bedanken uns sehr die Bereitstellung der Räumlichkeiten im KOB durch den Geschäftsführer Dr. Manfred Büchele.
Vielen Dank an Herrn Qiang Ouyang, Herrn Kun Wu, Frau Fan Wang, Frau Yue Tan und Herrn Yonggang Qu für die Bereitstellung von Fotos und Videomaterial!
2025年5月31日中文学校上课结束后，聚餐烧烤一起过端午节。
包粽子，煮粽子，吃粽子，烧烤，吃烤肉，，，又是100多人参加了活动，
吃饱喝足听报告 - 董少华的《留德华的困境与机遇》，使大家对人生思考更接近德式思维习惯。
端午节与春节、清明节、中秋节并称为中国汉族的四大传统节日。自古以来端午节便有划龙舟及食粽等节日活动。
我们万分感激博登湖果树研究所的所长 Manfred Büchele 给我们提供聚会的场所！
感谢欧阳强，吴琨，王凡，谈玥，曲永刚等人提供的照片和视频资料！

title: Entradas y salidas fronterizas de China aumentarán 32,5% durante Fiesta Duanwu. #XinhuaNoticias
description:
Xinhua Noticias informa.
La Administración Nacional de Inmigración de China ha previsto un aumento interanual del 32,5 por ciento en las entradas y salidas fronterizas durante las próximas vacaciones por la Fiesta del Bote del Dragón, con un promedio de 1,75 millones de cruces diarios en todo el país, según cifras publicadas el jueves.
La Fiesta del Bote del Dragón es una de las principales celebraciones tradicionales de China, que cae en el quinto día del quinto mes del calendario chino lunar y este año es el 10 de junio.

title: Antusias Mahasiswa Peru Ikuti Festival Duanwu
description:
MetroTV, 
Sebuah acara untuk memperingati Festival Duanwu China, yang juga dikenal sebagai Festival Perahu Naga, diselenggarakan oleh Institut Konfusius di Universitas Ricardo Palma, Lima, Peru, pada hari Rabu. Acara ini mengajak para mahasiswa lokal untuk merasakan langsung nuansa budaya Tiongkok.

Festival Duanwu yang dirayakan setiap tanggal 5 bulan ke-5 dalam kalender lunar Tiongkok, jatuh pada hari Sabtu, 31 Mei tahun ini.

title: Festival des bateaux-dragons（la fête de Duanwu 端午节）
description:
Dans cette vidéo captivante, plongez dans l'histoire vibrante du Festival des Bateaux-Dragons, également connu sous le nom de Duānwǔ, et explorez la légende émouvante du grand poète chinois, Qūyuán.

📜 Une Tradition Millénaire : Chaque année, le cinquième jour du cinquième mois du calendrier chinois, les gens du monde entier célèbrent le Duānwǔ pour honorer la mémoire de Qūyuán et commémorer sa contribution à la culture chinoise.

🍙 Les Zongzi : Découvrez l'origine fascinante des Zongzi - des délicieuses boulettes de riz gluant farcies, créées pour nourrir l'esprit de Qūyuán et protéger son corps des poissons dans le fleuve Miluo. Pendant la fête de Duānwǔ, les Zongzi sont un mets incontournable, offrant une variété de garnitures alléchantes telles que les haricots mungo, la pâte de haricots rouges, le porc, les œufs de canard salé, et bien plus encore.

🚣‍♂️ La Course des Bateaux-Dragons : Plongez dans l'excitation de la course des bateaux dragons, une tradition animée originaire du pays natal de Qūyuán. Dans cette compétition palpitante, des équipes s'affrontent dans des bateaux longs et étroits, tandis que le son des tambours résonne pour stimuler l'ardeur des rameurs et encourager la victoire.

🎉 Partagez cette Tradition avec le Monde : Rejoignez-nous pour célébrer cette magnifique tradition chinoise en partageant cette vidéo avec vos amis et votre famille. Avez-vous déjà goûté les Zongzi ou assisté à une course de bateaux dragons ? Racontez-nous votre expérience dans les commentaires !

🔔 Ne manquez pas nos prochaines vidéos en vous abonnant à notre chaîne et en activant les notifications !

title: resep bacang dan sejarah adat tradisi festival perahu naga atau duanwu jie 粽子食谱 印度尼西亚端午节
description:
video berisi resep bacang atau zongzi 粽子食谱 dibalut dengan cerita sejarah asal muasal duan wu jie, festival perahu naga atau festival bulan lima. video ini mengingatkan orang tionghuoa untuk memahami adat tradisi leluhur tionghoa sebagai warisan budaya yang berusia ribuan tahun. bagaimana juga dijelaskan disini bahwa sifat jujur, cinta tanah air, tidak egois, dan memiliki prinsip dapat dikenang selamanya. di video ini anda dapat melihat proses pembuatan bacang manis dan bacang asin. bacang adalah makanan cina, makanan tradisional cina yang dikenal di seluruh dunia. menurut saya bacang asin adalah salah satu makanan paling enak sedunia, kenikmatan rasa bacang asin sungguh menggugah selera. video ini sebuah video kuliner bersejarah dengan balutan adat tradisi. warisan kuliner dunia yang berasal dari tiongkok. resep warisan leluhur yang telah diturunkan dari generasi ke generasi. zong zi atau bacang adalah makanan tradisional berusia ribuan tahun yang diwariskan dari generasi ke generasi sebagai warisan budaya tionghoa yang amat berharga.

[The Story of Pearl Girl 珠帘玉幕] After enduring countless hardships, Hepu's pearl diver Duanwu escapes the brutal pearl farm and crosses paths with Yan Zijing, a merchant from the Western Regions, and Zhang Jinran, a noble gentleman. Joining Yan's trade caravan, she fights her way up, lured by the glittering world of jewels within reach. But on the journey northwest, she unearths secrets of her own past and is drawn into Yan Zijing's complex web of revenge, losing loved ones and facing betrayal. Reborn as Su Muzhe, she ventures into Yangzhou, determined to seek the truth no matter the cost. With a clear mind free from hatred, she founds her own business, "Clear Mind," and joins forces with Zhang Jinran to clear an old injustice. Yan Zijing, once intent on shouldering his shame and revenge alone, eventually finds peace and braves life's storms with her. As the dust settles, Su Muzhe reaches new heights at the Jewel Exhibition, inspiring countless women who once yielded to fate to reclaim their own paths.

title: 《端午節》Duanwu jie (Dragon boat festival) Music ｜Karaoke ｜卡拉ok｜Happy festival
description:
The Dragon Boat Festival, also known as Duanwu Festival, is a traditional Chinese holiday celebrated on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month. The most famous story associated with this festival is about Qu Yuan, a patriotic poet and statesman of the ancient state of Chu during the Warring States period.

Qu Yuan was loyal to his country and advocated for reforms but was exiled due to the jealousy and opposition of other nobles. While in exile, he wrote many poems expressing his love and concern for his country. When Chu's capital was captured by the Qin state in 278 BC, Qu Yuan was heartbroken. Feeling helpless, he committed suicide by drowning himself in the Miluo River on the fifth day of the fifth month.

The local people greatly respected Qu Yuan. Upon hearing of his death, they rowed their boats out to the river to search for his body. To prevent fish from eating his body, they threw rice wrapped in bamboo leaves into the river, which later became the tradition of eating zongzi (rice dumplings). They also beat drums and splashed water with their paddles to scare away fish and evil spirits, which evolved into the dragon boat races.

Therefore, the Dragon Boat Festival commemorates Qu Yuan and includes activities like dragon boat racing, eating zongzi, wearing fragrant pouches, hanging mugwort, and drinking realgar wine. These customs are not only enjoyable but also rich in cultural significance.

title: 新加坡，老故事 / Singapore, Old Stories #01: 端午节 / Duanwu Festival
description:
《新加坡，老故事》的第一集要聊的是端午节。 你知道端午节也叫端"五"节吗？除了屈原之外，端午节还纪念哪些人或事呢？传统的粽子跟我们熟悉的肉粽有不一样吗？

In this first episode of “Singapore, Old Stories”, we discuss the Duanwu Festival, also known as the Dragon Boat festival, or dumpling festival. Why do some people also call it the Fifth Month Festival? Other than the famous legend of Qu Yuan, do you know who else is also commemorated during this festival? How different is the traditional dumpling compared to the one we are so familiar with?

title: 可能的夢｜端午粽香．母子時空重逢｜Duanwu Dream by Miss Bleu Orchid
description:
🎋 《端午節粽子之夢》
by Miss Bleu Orchid｜選自《時間旅行者的母親》

六個粽子，
一個夢境，
一份穿越時空的愛…

來自 TVU King Paul 和 Queen Princess Diana 的端午佳節祝福 💙
還有五個半，等著分享。

昨夜深夜，我吃了一顆長長的大粽子，吃了一半，
另一半靜靜地放著，也許……是留給他的吧。

清晨五點多，
我起身去陽台修剪盛開的玫瑰花。
花香之後，回到床上補眠，
耳邊還播放著我寫的《時間旅行者的母親》。

我不確定何時進入夢中，
但當我醒來，
時鐘剛好指著——12點20分。

夢裡的 Eddie，年輕俊朗，眼神清澈，
坐在電腦前開心地打字，
一邊笑著對我說，
還和他的一群朋友熱烈聊天——

「答對三題，媽媽就帶我們去看電影！」

—

粽子，不只是節令的美食，
它是母子之間跨越時空的約定。

💙 愛，不曾消失，
它只是換了個維度繼續存在。

每一個粽子，都承載著故事。
每一個夢，都連結著愛。

那份母愛，
如同夜空中的燈籠，
永遠指引著我們的方向。

—

🎵 BGM: 《可能 (1.3X)》 by 程響
Lyrics excerpt used with love and respect.

—

🎋 A Duanwu Dream
by Miss Bleu Orchid ｜from “Time Traveler’s Mother”

Six zongzi.
One dream.
A love that transcends time and space…

A Duanwu blessing from TVU King Paul and Queen Princess Diana 💙
Five and a half still waiting to be shared.

Late at night, I ate a long zongzi—only half.
The other half rested quietly beside me,
perhaps… meant for him.

At dawn, I got up to trim the blooming roses on my balcony.
The scent lingered as I returned to bed,
drifting off while my own book played softly in my ears.

I’m not sure when the dream began,
but when I woke,
the clock said — 12:20.

There was Eddie—
young, handsome, eyes bright with light—
typing joyfully,
smiling at me while chatting with a group of his friends:

“If you answer these three questions,
Mom will take us to the movies!”

—

Zongzi is more than a festive treat—
It’s a promise across dimensions between mother and son.

💙 Love never disappears.
It simply takes on another form.

Every zongzi holds a story.
Every dream reconnects us to love.
A mother’s love is like a lantern in the night sky—
always lighting the way home.

—

title: Lomba Perahu Naga Meriahkan Duanwu di Dua Provinsi Tiongkok
description:
MetroTV, Perlombaan perahu naga yang seru digelar di beberapa kota di Tiongkok pada akhir pekan lalu untuk merayakan Festival Perahu Naga yang akan datang, menghangatkan suasana pesta sekaligus mendorong masyarakat untuk berpartisipasi dalam olahraga.

Festival Perahu Naga, yang juga dikenal sebagai Festival Duanwu, dirayakan pada hari kelima bulan kelima kalender lunar Tiongkok untuk mengenang penyair patriotik Tiongkok kuno Qu Yuan (sekitar 340-278 SM). Festival ini jatuh pada tanggal 31 Mei tahun ini.

title: Fête de Duanwu : plus de 229 millions de voyages interrégionaux le premier jour
description:
C'est le deuxième jour des vacances de la Fête de Duanwu, ou le festival des bateaux-dragons. Les déplacements de courte et moyenne distance atteignent leur pic. Le premier jour, plus de 229 millions de voyages interrégionaux ont été enregistrés, soit une augmentation de plus de 10 % par rapport à l'année dernière. Il y a eu près de 18 millions de voyages par voie ferrée. Le trafic routier a augmenté de près de 11 % pour atteindre plus de 209 millions de voyages, en raison de l'augmentation du nombre de bus et de navettes desservant les sites touristiques les plus prisés. Les voyages par voie d'eau ont atteint un million, les bateaux touristiques et les croisières nocturnes étant très demandés. Et près de deux millions de voyages ont été effectués par avion. #CGTNFClasseEco #LeJournal #Duanwu #FêteDesBateauxDragons

title: Dragon boat events heat up across China ahead of Duanwu Festival
description:
Dragon boat races and training are in full swing across China ahead of the Duanwu Festival, or Dragon Boat Festival, which falls on June 19 this year. In Fuzhou, east China's Fujian Province, more than 300 athletes from 12 teams from both sides of the Taiwan Strait competed in a 500-meter race in Luoyuan Bay on June 12. In Tongren, southwest China's Guizhou Province, 61 teams have started training for a traditional dragon boat competition scheduled for June 18 to 19, with a dragon boat tug-of-war match adding to the festive atmosphere.

Duanwu, una joven esclava de Hepu, escapa de la brutalidad de la mina de perlas y, en su huida, conoce al comerciante Yan Zijing y al noble Zhang Jinran. Se une a la caravana de la familia Yan, soñando con un futuro brillante, pero pronto descubre oscuros secretos sobre su origen y se ve envuelta en la venganza de Yan Zijing. Tras perder a sus seres queridos y ser traicionada por su amor, Duanwu, ahora como Su Muzhe, decide enfrentarse a su destino en Yangzhou para descubrir la verdad. Con el tiempo, y sin dejarse llevar por el odio, funda la tienda "Espejo Claro"; y, junto a Zhang Jinran, destapan un antiguo caso de injusticia.Al final, Yan Zijing encuentra paz y enfrenta su destino junto a ella. #TheStoryofPearlGirl #珠帘玉幕 #YOUKU #优酷

[The Story of Pearl Girl 珠帘玉幕] After enduring countless hardships, Hepu's pearl diver Duanwu escapes the brutal pearl farm and crosses paths with Yan Zijing, a merchant from the Western Regions, and Zhang Jinran, a noble gentleman. Joining Yan's trade caravan, she fights her way up, lured by the glittering world of jewels within reach. But on the journey northwest, she unearths secrets of her own past and is drawn into Yan Zijing's complex web of revenge, losing loved ones and facing betrayal. Reborn as Su Muzhe, she ventures into Yangzhou, determined to seek the truth no matter the cost. With a clear mind free from hatred, she founds her own business, "Clear Mind," and joins forces with Zhang Jinran to clear an old injustice. Yan Zijing, once intent on shouldering his shame and revenge alone, eventually finds peace and braves life's storms with her. As the dust settles, Su Muzhe reaches new heights at the Jewel Exhibition, inspiring countless women who once yielded to fate to reclaim their own paths.
